audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition with Jeremiah 31 citation from Greek Scripture; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for heavenly high priest, true tent, copy/shadow language, better covenant, Jeremiah new covenant citation.

verses.restored: Now the main point of what is being said: we have such a high priest, who sat at right hand of throne of Majesty in the heavens,
minister of the holy places and of the true tent, which the Lord pitched, not a human.
For every high priest is appointed to offer both gifts and sacrifices; therefore it is necessary also for this one to have something to offer.
If indeed he were upon earth, he would not be priest, since there are those offering gifts according to Law,

who serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things, just as Moses was warned when about to complete the tent: See, he says, that you make all things according to the pattern shown to you on the mountain.
But now he has obtained a more excellent service, by as much as he is mediator of a better covenant, which has been legislated upon better promises.
For if that first had been faultless, no place would have been sought for a second.
For finding fault with them, he says: Look, days are coming, says Lord, and I will complete upon the house of Israel and upon the house of Judah a new covenant,

not according to the covenant that I made with their ancestors in the day of taking hold of their hand to lead them out of land of Egypt, because they did not remain in my covenant, and I disregarded them, says Lord.

Because this is the covenant that I will covenant with the house of Israel after those days, says Lord: giving my laws into their mind, I will also write them upon their hearts; and I will be to them Divine, and they will be to me people.
And they shall not teach each one one's citizen and each one one's sibling, saying, Know the Lord, because all will know me, from small to great of them.
Because I will be merciful to their injustices, and their sins/misalignments I will certainly remember no more.
In saying new, he has made the first old; but what is becoming old and aging is near disappearance.

translation_notes: New covenant is quoted from Jeremiah to Israel and Judah; do not erase its Jewish addressees.
Copy/shadow language describes cultic pattern relation, not contempt for earthly temple or Jewish worship.
The covenant promise centers internal Torah, knowing the Lord, mercy, and non-remembrance of sins.
safety_note: Hebrews 8 is often misused supersessionistically. The source text itself quotes a covenant promised to house of Israel and house of Judah; restoration must preserve that continuity.
